user_query:
I’m a middle-school history teacher who loves historical-themed games. Please build and run a small, reproducible pipeline that turns the topics in input/game_topics.csv into a validated mini question bank. I need you to: (1) perform live web searches for each topic using the hint_keywords, (2) extract structured facts from reputable pages, (3) consolidate the facts with simple validation, and (4) generate multiple-choice questions. Deliverables and constraints:

Inputs (provided):
- Read input/game_topics.csv.

Internet use (essential):
- For each row’s hint_keywords, use a web search engine to find at least two distinct reputable sources. Only count sources from these allowed domains: britannica.com; bbc.co.uk; loc.gov; si.edu or smithsonianmag.com; nationalgeographic.com; or any domain ending in .edu. Do not hardcode URLs in advance—discover them via search. Do not manually paste web text; the script must perform the searches and downloads at runtime.

Required script and run command:
- Implement scripts/build_question_bank.py that, when run, reads input/game_topics.csv, performs the searches and downloads, extracts facts, validates them, and writes all outputs below.
- Run it once and capture stdout to a file using this exact command:
  python3 scripts/build_question_bank.py input/game_topics.csv out/validated_facts.csv out/mcq_questions.csv out/web_sources.jsonl > out/run_summary.txt

Required outputs (create the out/ folder if needed):
1) out/web_sources.jsonl
   - One JSON object per line per (topic_id, source) with fields:
     topic_id (int), topic_title (str), source_url (str), source_domain (str), allowed_domain (bool), sha256 (str of raw HTML), extracted_year_start (int or null), extracted_year_end (int or null), extracted_place (str; can be empty), extracted_key_figure (str; can be empty), retrieval_timestamp (ISO 8601 string).
   - Only sources with allowed_domain=true count toward the minimum of two per topic.

2) out/validated_facts.csv
   - One row per topic with fields:
     topic_id, topic_title, year_start (int), year_end (int), place (non-empty str), key_figure (str; may be empty except where required), source_count (int), source_urls (semicolon-separated list of allowed sources used).
   - Validation rules you must satisfy using the data in out/web_sources.jsonl:
     • For each topic, source_count must be ≥ 2 (count only allowed_domain=true entries).
     • year_start must equal the minimum of all non-null extracted_year_start/ extracted_year_end among allowed sources for that topic; year_end must equal the maximum of those values.
     • For topics whose prompt_focus includes "key_figure" (see input CSV), key_figure in validated_facts.csv must equal one of the non-empty extracted_key_figure values present in out/web_sources.jsonl for that topic.

3) out/mcq_questions.csv
   - Exactly two questions per topic (total 10), each focusing on factual checks tied to the topic’s prompt_focus (e.g., one about the time period and one about location or key figure). Fields:
     id (unique), topic_id, question, option_a, option_b, option_c, option_d, correct_option (A/B/C/D), explanation, source_urls (semicolon-separated allowed sources used).
   - The explanation must cite facts reflected in out/validated_facts.csv, and source_urls must be a subset of those in out/validated_facts.csv for that topic.

4) out/run_summary.txt
   - Captured stdout from the run must include:
     • A line: TOTAL_TOPICS: 5
     • One line per topic in the form: TOPIC <topic_id>: allowed_sources=<number>

Notes:
- Extract structured facts from the downloaded web pages (HTML) such as years and places using your chosen approach. Keep the pipeline deterministic by relying on the local artifacts you produce for validation (e.g., min/max year logic).
- Do not use paywalled or login-only sources. Do not rely on social media or forums.

Acceptance checks I will use locally:
- All output files exist with the specified paths and required fields.
- For each topic, web_sources.jsonl has ≥ 2 lines with allowed_domain=true and distinct sha256 values.
- validated_facts.csv year_start/year_end are consistent with min/max across the corresponding allowed sources in web_sources.jsonl, and source_count matches the number of allowed sources listed.
- mcq_questions.csv contains exactly 10 questions (2 per topic), each referencing source_urls that are a subset of the allowed sources used for that topic.
- out/run_summary.txt contains the required TOTAL_TOPICS line and five per-topic lines with allowed_sources counts. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
